data.title: Sezzle stock down after director resigns on 'growing difference' with management
data.description:
Sezzle (SEZL) was trading lower after the fintech announced the resignation of Board Director Karen Webster.

Shares were 12.49% down Friday morning to $60.49.

The resignation, effective immediately, resulted from "a growing difference in perspective with management concerning the company's direction, key decisions, and governance".

Webster had also resigned as a member of the audit and risk committee, compensation committee, and nominating and corporate governance committee of the board.

Bryan Hunt has been appointed a successor to fill the vacancy.

Hunt is set to serve as a non-employee director until Sezzle's next annual meeting of stockholders and until a successor is duly elected.

Hunt is a general partner of Relevance Ventures. Prior to that, he spent over 24 years at Wells Fargo (WFC) and its predecessors in both high yield and equity research.

data.title: Petrobras to regain full ownership of key Campos Basin assets in $450M deal
data.description:
Petrobras (PBR) said Friday it agreed to acquire the remaining 50% stakes in the Tartaruga Verde and Espadarte Module 3 fields in Brazil's offshore Campos Basin from Malaysia's Petronas for $450M; upon closing, Petrobras will hold 100% ownership while continuing as operator of both assets.

Petrobras (PBR) currently produces ~55K bbl/day of oil from the two fields through the Cidade de Campos dos Goytacazes floating production, storage and offloading vessel.

The acquisition marks another step in Petrobras' (PBR) ongoing strategy to consolidate control over high-margin offshore assets, particularly in the mature but still prolific Campos Basin.

Separately, Petrobras (PBR) said ‌it will reimburse fuel distributors that had won a liquefied petroleum gas auction in March, paying ​them the difference between their bids ​and import prices at the time.

Brazil's President ⁠Luiz Inacio Lula da Silva said last week ​the government would seek to annul the auction to ​protect consumers, arguing the company had sold LPG in the auction at prices he believed were too ​high due to the Middle East war.

data.title: SA analyst upgrades/downgrades: NFLX, LUV, XOM, AMZN
data.description:
Agar Capital has upgraded Amazon (AMZN) as AI enthusiasm returns to the market, while Dhierin Bechai sees opportunity in Southwest Airlines (LUV) despite oil price headwinds. On the downgrade front, Asian Value Investor has moved Exxon Mobil (XOM) to a more cautious stance on valuation concerns, and Vinay Utham, CFA, has tempered expectations for Netflix (NFLX) ahead of its Q1 earnings report.

Upgrades

  • Amazon.com (AMZN): Upgrade to Buy by Agar Capital. The analyst cites improved risk/reward dynamics and clearer cash flow visibility from AWS, advertising, and retail segments, with AI infrastructure and same-day delivery expansion reinforcing Amazon’s competitive position.

    “The market became spooked by the enormous capital expenditure forecasts, but the company is not recklessly burning cash. Instead, the company is funding its AI expansion through the cash generated by two very high-growth areas: e-commerce and advertising.”

  • Southwest Airlines Co. (LUV): Upgrade Hold to Buy by Dhierin Bechai. Despite headwinds from higher oil prices and inflation, the analyst believes strategic cost management, bag fee hikes, and potential fare increases can offset these pressures and deliver meaningful upside.

    “It is not the case that Southwest Airlines just increases the ticket prices and bag fees and then it is business as usual. The supply tightening should provide stability to unit revenues, and at the same time, the company has to take variable costs out of the business.”

Downgrades

  • Exxon Mobil (XOM): Downgrade Buy to Hold by Asian Value Investor. The analyst notes that the current valuation fully reflects both upside and downside from geopolitical risks, with technical indicators flagging overbought conditions and limited room for appreciation.

    “With regard to its geographical exposure, XOM faces some risks because it has assets in the Middle East. In fact, it lost 6% of its global oil and gas production during Q1 2026 amid the Iran war. This might lead to lower sales volume in the first half of 2026. But I think this can still be offset by the sharp increase in oil prices.”

  • Netflix (NFLX): Downgrade Buy to Hold by Vinay Utham, CFA. The analyst views Netflix as fairly valued heading into Q1 earnings, with a symmetric risk/reward profile that leaves no cushion for potential disappointments despite strong underlying fundamentals.

    “The downgrade merely reflects the fact that at current levels, the stock is already pricing in the Q1 ad-revenue growth scenario, leaving no cushion for any disappointment. The risk/reward becomes more favourable at the mid-$80s.”

data.title: The U.S. Strategic Petroleum Reserve's risky descent into the 1980s
data.description:
The U.S. is releasing oil from its strategic reserve as part of a coordinated effort by the International Energy Agency to help ease supply pressures caused by the Iran war, at the risk of significantly depleting its stockpile. 

The Department of Energy is targeting to release 172M barrels from the Strategic Petroleum Reserve (SPR).

This week, the DOE issued a solicitation for bids for an emergency exchange of up to 30M bbls of sweet crude as part of the planned SPR release.

This is the third such solicitation so far, bringing the overall emergency exchanges to 55M bbls. Under the terms of the exchange, companies will return the borrowed oil to the DOE with additional barrels as a premium.

The 172M bbls that the U.S. is targeting to release represented 41% of the overall SPR level last month. The inventory as of April 3 stood at 413.3M bbls.

Once the IEA-coordinated release is complete, the SPR is expected to have ~243M bbls of oil left – a level not seen since the early 1980s.

To note, the Biden administration drew down 180M bbls from the SPR to stabilize oil prices after Russia invaded Ukraine in 2022. The move led to SPR levels reaching as low as ~346M bbls in mid-2023.

Last month, Energy Secretary Chris Wright said the U.S. arranged to refill the SPR with around 200M bbls within the next year. That's 20% more barrels than will be drawn down.

Take a look at how SPR levels have changed since the 1980s and how much the level is projected to drop after this year's planned release:

data.title: Nexstar jumps as Citi sees better chance of upside
data.description:
Nexstar Media stock (NXST) was up more than 5% Friday morning after an upgrade to Buy at Citi, which saw the stock come under pressure amid bumps in integrating the newly acquired Tegna.

Nexstar closed its $6.2B acquisition of Tegna March 19, set to establish a new power in local TV station ownership. But the deal came under immediate legal pressure amid litigation from DirecTV and eight state attorneys general, resulting in a temporary restraining order halting the companies' integration.

Looking at the current risks and potential rewards, though, analyst Jason Bazinet saw significantly more upside potential than downside.

"We sized three risks: (1) reduced synergies post potential station divestitures, (2) stations sales at a lower multiple relative to the Tegna purchase price, and (3) a potential recession (given the Middle East conflict)," Bazinet wrote. 

"From prevailing levels, we see $8 of potential downside and $73 of potential upside, a 9:1 risk-reward."

The legal case could result in station divestitures, he noted, either in the eight states represented by the AGs or in all states where Nexstar has heavy share. And that introduces two risks: reduced synergies, and sales at a lower multiple than the Tegna purchase price.

But "We believe Nexstar’s stock could reach $252 per share if Tegna proceeds unscathed," Bazinet wrote.

The firm upgraded Nexstar but cut its target price to $220 (implying 17% upside) on the assumption that Nexstar sells stations covering 25M homes but that the U.S. doesn't enter a recession.

data.title: There are now more overbought stocks than oversold in the S&P 500
data.description:
After seven straight days of gains, there are now more overbought stocks in the S&P 500 (SP500) than oversold stocks.

The percentage of overbought stocks amounted to 21.8%, compared to 14.4% for oversold stocks.

 

 

 

Below are the 10 stocks with the highest relative strength index within the S&P 500 (SP500).

The Relative Strength Index (or RSI) measures the speed and magnitude of price changes to identify overbought or oversold conditions. A measure above 70 signals overbought conditions, and below 30 signals oversold conditions. It also helps determine if an asset is set for a reversal or correction.

  1. Avis Budget Group (CAR) – RSI: 92
  2. TD SYNNEX Corp. (SNX) – RSI: 80
  3. Smithfield Foods (SFD) – RSI: 78
  4. Entergy Corp. (ETR) – RSI: 76
  5. Marvell Technology (MRVL) – RSI: 76
  6. Intel Corp. (INTC) – RSI: 75
  7. Equinix (EQIX) – RSI: 74
  8. The Bank of New York Mellon Corp. (BK) – RSI: 73
  9. Brown-Forman Corp. (BF.B) – RSI: 72
  10. Monolithic Power Systems (MPWR) – RSI: 72

S&P 500 ETFs: (SPY), (VOO), (IVV), (RSP), (SSO), (UPRO), (SH), (SDS), (SPXU), (FXAIX), (VFIAX), (VFFSX), and (SWPPX).

data.title: Stryker confident of 2026 outlook despite Q1 impact from cyberattack
data.description:
Stryker (SYK) has announced that the company remains confident in meeting its 2026 financial guidance despite a material impact on its operations and a potential hit to its Q1 financials due to the recent cybersecurity incident.

In a statement on Thursday, U.S. MedTech disclosed that its global manufacturing network is fully operational, and it has restored its business functions, including ordering and distribution systems, following the event.

Shares of Stryker (SYK) fell last month after a pro-Palestinian hacking group carried out the cyberattack, disrupting its information systems and business applications based on Microsoft’s (MSFT) Windows operating systems.

“The company has determined that the incident had a material impact on its operations, with resulting impact to the company’s financial results for the first quarter of 2026,” SYK said, adding that its investigation into the incident is ongoing.

However, “the incident has not had, and is not reasonably likely to have, a material impact on the company’s 2026 full-year guidance,” according to Stryker (SYK).

data.title: FGI outlines 2026 revenue guidance of $134M-$141M amid tariff uncertainty
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: FGI Industries Ltd. (FGI) Q4 2025

Management View

  • “Our fourth quarter results reflect the strategic investments we’ve made in our organic growth initiatives across our brands, products and channels or BPC strategy,” said CEO David Bruce, while noting “the broader industry navigated a fluid environment following the Supreme Court decision in February and subsequent tariff actions.”
  • “When evaluating our fourth quarter revenue of $30.5 million, it is important to consider 2 primary factors: prior year comparatives” and “tariff headwinds,” with Bruce adding that tariff-driven uncertainty “impacted volumes in our sanitaryware and shower systems businesses despite positive underlying demand trends.”
  • “Our gross margin expanded by 210 basis points to 26.7% this quarter, driven by the better relative performance of our higher-margin businesses,” Bruce said, alongside a portfolio message: “we continue to high-grade our portfolio.”
  • “While we saw temporary revenue pressure in the U.S., Canada and Europe, our geographic expansion into India and our continued growth in Covered Bridge Kitchen Cabinetry hold significant promise for driving growth in the coming quarters,” Bruce said.
  • “We are providing our 2026 guidance as follows: our revenue guidance is $134 million to $141 million,” said CFO Jae Chung.

Outlook

  • “We are providing our 2026 guidance as follows: our revenue guidance is $134 million to $141 million,” Chung said, adding, “The adjusted operating income guidance is $0.7 million to $2.5 million. The adjusted net income guidance is a loss of $0.3 million to a positive $1.1 million.”
  • “Please note that the guidance for adjusted operating income excludes certain nonrecurring items,” Chung said, and “Adjusted net income excludes certain nonrecurring items and includes an adjustment for minority interest.”
  • Prior-quarter comparison was not possible because the prior-quarter transcript was not provided beyond “not available.”

Financial Results

  • “For the fourth quarter of 2025, revenue totaled $30.5 million, a decrease of 14.4% compared to the fourth quarter of 2024,” Chung said.
  • “Gross profit was $8.1 million in the quarter, a decrease of 6.8% year-over-year,” Chung said, adding, “Our gross margin increased to 26.7% in the quarter compared to 24.6% in the prior year.”
  • “Our operating expenses decreased to $8.8 million compared to $10 million in the prior year period due primarily to optimizing our warehouse operations,” Chung said.
  • “GAAP operating loss was $0.7 million, improving from an operating loss of $1.3 million in the prior year period,” Chung said, and attributed the change to “a decrease in selling and distribution costs as well as lower R&D costs.”
  • “GAAP net loss attributable to shareholders was $2.6 million compared to a net loss of $0.4 million in the same period last year,” Chung said, adding, “Excluding these items, adjusted net loss for the fourth quarter of 2025 was $0.6 million versus an adjusted net loss of $0.7 million in the same prior year period.”
  • “At the end of the fourth quarter, FGI had $8.5 million in total liquidity,” Chung said.

Q&A

  • John Joseph McGlade, The Benchmark Company, LLC: “what assumptions in the end markets you’re assuming relative to the full year guide?” CEO Bruce: “we feel really comfortable in all of our broader category momentum,” while also saying “there’s still a lot of uncertainty out there,” citing “the trade and tariff situation” and “the war in the Middle East.”
  • John Joseph McGlade, The Benchmark Company, LLC: “what demand has looked like year-to-date” and “do you anticipate that you or your customers would be the ones to benefit from any refunds if they were to come?” CEO Bruce: “we like what we’re seeing so far in Q1 based on our expectations and based on what we’ve built into our guide,” and on refunds, “I think that’s way too early to tell.”
  • Gregory Gibas, Northland Capital Markets: “elaborate… the pickup inactivity… in Q1 of 2026 versus… Q4.” CEO Bruce: “we faced… a lot of order pull forward” in Q4 2024 and later “a real whipsaw effect” as customers “pause[d] orders” amid tariff impacts.
  • Gregory Gibas, Northland Capital Markets: progress on “China Plus One strategy.” CEO Bruce: “we’ve made some really good progress,” and “have secured some additional partnerships outside of China,” referencing “sources such as Thailand.”
  • Gregory Gibas, Northland Capital Markets: growth opportunities and “new program launches… in ’26.” CEO Bruce: “the addition of the dealers… continue to grow” in India, with Delhi penetration starting, and highlighted “a new wholesale bath initiative in Germany,” saying “that’s been going very, very well for the last 8 to 10 months.”

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts: slightly positive and detail-seeking, including “congratulations on the quarter” (Benchmark) while pressing on tariffs, demand visibility, and sourcing diversification.
  • Management: slightly positive in prepared remarks, with caution in Q&A, including “we feel really comfortable” alongside “there’s still a lot of uncertainty out there,” and “that’s way too early to tell.”
  • Prior-quarter tone comparison was not possible because the prior-quarter transcript was “not available.”

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • A comparison to the prior quarter could not be produced because the prior-quarter transcript was not available.

Risks and Concerns

  • “The industry outlook remains uncertain due to the current tariff environment,” Bruce said, adding that tariffs “impacted volumes” in sanitaryware and shower systems.
  • Bruce said the company was planning for ongoing policy volatility: “We fully expect… a lot of the tariffs… most likely will come back in other forms and sectorial tariffs as the year goes on.”
  • On mitigation, Bruce said FGI “secured some additional partnerships outside of China” to “help lessen the impact of the uncertainties” and cited “Thailand” as part of diversification.

Final Takeaway

Management framed Q4 results around tariff-driven volatility and difficult comparisons, while emphasizing margin expansion, lower operating expenses, and a 2026 guide that includes $134 million to $141 million in revenue and adjusted operating income of $0.7 million to $2.5 million. In Q&A, executives repeatedly stressed uncertainty around tariffs and geopolitics, while pointing to early Q1 demand tracking to internal expectations, sourcing diversification efforts outside China, and growth initiatives in India, Germany, and expanded U.S. wholesale distribution.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: Palantir declines, but Wedbush downplays any threat by Anthropic
data.description:
Palantir (PLTR) shares were down 3% during early Friday trading, as the not-so-long-ago AI and analytics darling has now plunged 29% year to date.

Wedbush contends the drop by Palantir and the software sector at large stems from Anthropic's (ANTHRO) momentum. However, Anthropic's success will not likely subtract from Palantir's business strides.

"While Anthropic is hitting a new scale with the company now at $30 billion ARR, up from $9 billion at the start of the year, we believe this is not at the expense of PLTR's business as the company continues to accelerate both its US commercial and government businesses, with US commercial growing 137% y/y and US government accelerating 66% y/y," Wedbush analysts said in a Friday investor report. 

Wedbush maintains its Outperform rating and $230 price target on Palantir.

"We acknowledge the growth from Anthropic is unprecedented, but we believe the take that Anthropic is eating PLTR's lunch (most recently amplified by Michael Burry's now-deleted post on X earlier today) is the wrong take and fictional narrative (in our view), as Palantir is at the epicenter of leaders in the AI Revolution while its AIP product moat remains unmatched in our view," Wedbush noted. "The data-driven moat by Palantir is around the data and its ontology…this is NOT being disrupted by Claude…if anything, it's accelerated on the enterprise."
